revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

{{神社 |画像 = |名称 = 志呂神社 |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]下神目1834 |位置 = |祭神 = [[事代主命]] ほか |神体 = |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = （伝）[[和銅]]6年（[[713年]]） |本殿 = [[中山造]]の発展形 |別名 = 志呂宮（しろみや） |例祭 = [[10月20日]]（秋祭） |神事 = 歳旦祭、輪潜祭 }} '''志呂神社'''（しろじんじゃ）は、[[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]下神目にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]である。'''志呂宮'''（しろみや）とも呼ばれる。福徳成就・安産・厄除けの福徳があるとされる。
== 祭神 == 祭神は、[[事代主命]]などである。
== 歴史 == === 創建 === [[美作国]]が[[備前国]]から分国した[[和銅]]6年（[[713年]]）に、[[弓削庄]]27カ村（現在の[[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]のうち旧[[久米郡]][[福渡町]]の範囲、および久米郡[[久米南町]]）の総氏神として祭られたと伝えられている。
== 境内 == * 本殿 : [[中山造]]より発展した3間4面（実尺は4間x4間）の総欅造の出向拝を備えた入母屋造妻入で、各所に彫物が施されている。[[1848年]]（[[嘉永]]元年）再建。<ref>現地の説明板による。</ref> * 三樹山（みきやま）の樹林 : 三樹山は、鎮守の森としてこれまでほとんど手が加えられることなく、自然の推移にゆだねられてきた。このため、シイノキ、ヤブツバキ、シリブカガシなど原始的な植生型に近い常緑広葉樹が育っている。常緑広葉樹林では、四季を通じて熱い葉に被われており、林外にくらべて気温の変化や湿度の変化が少なく、冬の寒さをきらう南方系と夏の暑さに弱い北方系の動植物が同居するめずらしいところである。[[1976年]]（昭和51年）に県指定郷土自然保護地域となった。<ref>現地の説明板による。</ref>
== 祭事 == * [[1月1日]]の歳旦祭 * 7月最終土曜日の輪潜祭 * [[10月20日]]の例祭（秋祭）
== 文化財 == === 重要無形民俗文化財（岡山県指定） === * 志呂神社御供 : 通称「京尾御供」と呼ばれ、志呂神社の秋祭（10月20日）に、[[久米南町]]京尾の氏子から供えられる神饌行事である。だんごでつくった「フト」と称する女陰を形どったもの3個と、男根を象徴した「マガリ」1個、もちでつくった「丁銀」3個、それに米飯一盛、柚子1個、箸1膳、榊葉若干を三方に盛った7膳の熟饌が調整され、御幣を立てた唐櫃にいれて御供所に運ばれ、紋付袴に榊葉を口にした7人の頭人によって供饌される。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）指定。<ref>現地の説明板による。</ref>
== 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]下神目1834
; 交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[津山線]]の[[福渡駅]]から、車で10分弱。 * [[国道53号線]]志呂神社前バス停から徒歩10分弱。
== 参考文献 == * 『建部町史』 * 現地説明板
==脚注== {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 志呂神社]（岡山県神社庁） * [http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html 志呂神社]（オープンエア・おかやま）

志呂神社の拝殿（手前）と本殿（奥） |名称 = 志呂神社 |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]下神目1834 |位置 = |祭神 = [[事代主命]]　ほか |神体 = |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = （伝）[[和銅]]6年（[[713年]]） |本殿 = [[中山造]]の発展形 |別名 = 志呂宮（しろみや） |例祭 = [[10月20日]]（秋祭） |神事 = 歳旦祭、輪潜祭 }} [[ファイル:Entarance_of_Shiro_Jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|志呂神社の表参道（随神門前）]] '''志呂神社'''（しろじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]下神目にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]である。'''志呂宮'''（しろみや）とも呼ばれる。福徳成就・安産・厄除けの福徳があるとされる。 == 祭神 == 祭神は、[[事代主命]]などである。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === [[美作国]]が[[備前国]]から分国した[[和銅]]6年（[[713年]]）に、[[弓削庄]]27カ村（現在の[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]のうち旧[[久米郡]][[福渡町]]の範囲、および久米郡[[久米南町]]）の総氏神として祭られたと伝えられている。 == 境内 == * 本殿 : [[中山造]]より発展した3間4面（実尺は4間×4間）の総欅造の出向拝を備えた入母屋造妻入で、各所に彫物が施されている。[[1848年]]（[[嘉永]]元年）再建現地の説明板による。。 * 三樹山（みきやま）の樹林 : 三樹山は、鎮守の森としてこれまでほとんど手が加えられることなく、自然の推移にゆだねられてきた。このため、シイノキ、ヤブツバキ、シリブカガシなど原始的な植生型に近い常緑広葉樹が育っている。常緑広葉樹林では、四季を通じて熱い葉に被われており、林外に比べて気温の変化や湿度の変化が少なく、冬の寒さをきらう南方系と夏の暑さに弱い北方系の動植物が同居する珍しいところである。[[1976年]]（昭和51年）に、岡山県指定郷土自然保護地域となった現地の説明板による。。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 輪潜祭（7月最終土曜日） * 例祭（秋祭、[[10月20日]]） == 文化財 == === 重要無形民俗文化財（岡山県指定） === * 志呂神社御供 : 通称「京尾御供」と呼ばれ、志呂神社の秋祭（10月20日）に、[[久米南町]]京尾の氏子から供えられる神饌行事である。だんごでつくった「フト」と称する女陰を形どったもの3個と、男根を象徴した「マガリ」1個、餅でつくった「丁銀」3個、それに米飯一盛、柚子1個、箸1膳、榊葉若干を三方に盛った7膳の熟饌が調整され、御幣を立てた唐櫃にいれて御供所に運ばれ、紋付袴に榊葉を口にした7人の頭人によって供饌される。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）指定現地の説明板による。。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]下神目1834 ; 交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[津山線]][[福渡駅]]から車で10分弱。 * [[国道53号]]志呂神社前バス停から徒歩10分弱。 == 参考文献 == * 『建部町史』 * 現地説明板 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 志呂神社]（岡山県神社庁） * [http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html 志呂神社]（オープンエア・おかやま） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しろしんしや}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形文化財]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]]

志呂神社の拝殿（手前）と本殿（奥） |名称 = 志呂神社 |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町下神目]]1834 |位置 = |祭神 = [[事代主命]]　ほか |神体 = |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = （伝）[[和銅]]6年（[[713年]]） |本殿 = [[中山造]]の発展形 |別名 = 志呂宮（しろみや） |例祭 = [[10月20日]]（秋祭） |神事 = 歳旦祭、輪潜祭 }} [[ファイル:Entarance_of_Shiro_Jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|志呂神社の表参道（随神門前）]] '''志呂神社'''（しろじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町下神目]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]である。'''志呂宮'''（しろみや）とも呼ばれる。福徳成就・安産・厄除けの福徳があるとされる。 == 祭神 == 祭神は、[[事代主命]]などである。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === [[美作国]]が[[備前国]]から分国した[[和銅]]6年（[[713年]]）に、[[弓削庄]]27カ村（現在の[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]のうち旧[[久米郡]][[福渡町]]の範囲、および久米郡[[久米南町]]）の総氏神として祭られたと伝えられている。 == 境内 == * 本殿 : [[中山造]]より発展した3間4面（実尺は4間×4間）の総欅造の出向拝を備えた入母屋造妻入で、各所に彫物が施されている。[[1848年]]（[[嘉永]]元年）再建現地の説明板による。。 * 三樹山（みきやま）の樹林 : 三樹山は、鎮守の森としてこれまでほとんど手が加えられることなく、自然の推移にゆだねられてきた。このため、シイノキ、ヤブツバキ、シリブカガシなど原始的な植生型に近い常緑広葉樹が育っている。常緑広葉樹林では、四季を通じて熱い葉に被われており、林外に比べて気温の変化や湿度の変化が少なく、冬の寒さをきらう南方系と夏の暑さに弱い北方系の動植物が同居する珍しいところである。[[1976年]]（昭和51年）に、岡山県指定郷土自然保護地域となった現地の説明板による。。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 輪潜祭（7月最終土曜日） * 例祭（秋祭、[[10月20日]]） == 文化財 == === 重要無形民俗文化財（岡山県指定） === * 志呂神社御供 : 通称「京尾御供」と呼ばれ、志呂神社の秋祭（10月20日）に、[[久米南町]]京尾の氏子から供えられる神饌行事である。だんごでつくった「フト」と称する女陰を形どったもの3個と、男根を象徴した「マガリ」1個、餅でつくった「丁銀」3個、それに米飯一盛、柚子1個、箸1膳、榊葉若干を三方に盛った7膳の熟饌が調整され、御幣を立てた唐櫃にいれて御供所に運ばれ、紋付袴に榊葉を口にした7人の頭人によって供饌される。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）指定現地の説明板による。。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]下神目1834 {{osm box|n|1907339715}} ; 交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[津山線]][[福渡駅]]から車で10分弱。 * [[国道53号]]志呂神社前バス停から徒歩10分弱。 == 参考文献 == * 『建部町史』 * 現地説明板 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 志呂神社]（岡山県神社庁） * [http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html 志呂神社]（オープンエア・おかやま） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しろしんしや}} [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形文化財]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]]

{{神社 |画像 = [[File:Shiro_Jinja.jpg|280px]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |画像 = [[File:Shiro_Jinja.jpg|280px]]
志呂神社の拝殿（手前）と本殿（奥） |名称 = 志呂神社 |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町下神目]]1834 |位置 = |祭神 = [[事代主命]]　ほか |神体 = |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = （伝）[[和銅]]6年（[[713年]]） |本殿 = [[中山造]]の発展形 |別名 = 志呂宮（しろみや） |例祭 = [[10月20日]]（秋祭） |神事 = 歳旦祭、輪潜祭 }} [[ファイル:Entarance_of_Shiro_Jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|志呂神社の表参道（随神門前）]] '''志呂神社'''（しろじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町下神目]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]である。'''志呂宮'''（しろみや）とも呼ばれる。福徳成就・安産・厄除けの福徳があるとされる。 == 祭神 == 祭神は、[[事代主命]]などである。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === [[美作国]]が[[備前国]]から分国した[[和銅]]6年（[[713年]]）に、[[弓削庄]]27カ村（現在の[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]のうち旧[[久米郡]][[福渡町]]の範囲、および久米郡[[久米南町]]）の総氏神として祭られたと伝えられている。 == 境内 == * 本殿 : [[中山造]]より発展した3間4面（実尺は4間×4間）の総欅造の出向拝を備えた入母屋造妻入で、各所に彫物が施されている。[[1848年]]（[[嘉永]]元年）再建現地の説明板による。。 * 三樹山（みきやま）の樹林 : 三樹山は、鎮守の森としてこれまでほとんど手が加えられることなく、自然の推移にゆだねられてきた。このため、シイノキ、ヤブツバキ、シリブカガシなど原始的な植生型に近い常緑広葉樹が育っている。常緑広葉樹林では、四季を通じて熱い葉に被われており、林外に比べて気温の変化や湿度の変化が少なく、冬の寒さをきらう南方系と夏の暑さに弱い北方系の動植物が同居する珍しいところである。[[1976年]]（昭和51年）に、岡山県指定郷土自然保護地域となった。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 輪潜祭（7月最終土曜日） * 例祭（秋祭、[[10月20日]]） == 文化財 == === 重要無形民俗文化財（岡山県指定） === * 志呂神社御供 : 通称「京尾御供」と呼ばれ、志呂神社の秋祭（10月20日）に、[[久米南町]]京尾の氏子から供えられる神饌行事である。だんごでつくった「フト」と称する女陰を形どったもの3個と、男根を象徴した「マガリ」1個、餅でつくった「丁銀」3個、それに米飯一盛、柚子1個、箸1膳、榊葉若干を三方に盛った7膳の熟饌が調整され、御幣を立てた唐櫃にいれて御供所に運ばれ、紋付袴に榊葉を口にした7人の頭人によって供饌される。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）指定。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町下神目]]1834 {{osm box|n|1907339715}} ; 交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[津山線]][[福渡駅]]から車で10分弱。 * [[国道53号]]志呂神社前バス停から徒歩10分弱。 == 参考文献 == * 『建部町史』 * 現地説明板 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 志呂神社]（岡山県神社庁） * {{Wayback|url=http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html |title=志呂神社 |date=*}}（オープンエア・おかやま） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しろしんしや}} [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形文化財]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Shiro Shrine |image = [[File:Shiro_Jinja.jpg|280px|alt=Shiro Shrine's Worship Hall and Main Hall]] |address = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = Kotoshironushi no Mikoto (and others) |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn Festival) |rituals = New Year's Festival, Ring Passing Festival }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined Deity == The primary enshrined deity is **Kotoshironushi no Mikoto**, among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * **Main Hall (Honden)**: Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * **Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest**: A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== Festivals and Rituals == * **New Year's Festival (January 1)** * **Ring Passing Festival (Last Saturday in July)** * **Autumn Festival (October 20)**: Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == ### Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation) * **Shiro Shrine Offerings**: Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn Festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * **Location**: Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * **Transport**: * Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. * A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is **Kotoshironushi no Mikoto**, among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * **main hall (Honden)**: Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * **Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest**: A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * **New Year's festival (January 1)** * **Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)** * **Autumn festival (October 20)**: Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == ### Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation) * **Shiro Shrine Offerings**: Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * **location**: Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * **Transport**: * Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. * A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is **Kotoshironushi no Mikoto**, among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * **main hall (Honden)**: Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * **Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest**: A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * **New Year's festival (January 1)** * **Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)** * **Autumn festival (October 20)**: Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == ### Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation) * **Shiro Shrine Offerings**: Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * **location**: Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * **Transport**: * Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. * A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shiro Shrine |image = [[File:Shiro_Jinja.jpg|280px|alt=Shiro Shrine's Worship Hall and main hall]] |location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = Kotoshironushi no Mikoto (and others) |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival) |rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is **Kotoshironushi no Mikoto**, among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * **main hall (Honden)**: Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * **Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest**: A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * **New Year's festival (January 1)** * **Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)** * **Autumn festival (October 20)**: Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == ### Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation) * **Shiro Shrine Offerings**: Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * **location**: Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * **Transport**: * Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. * A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shiro Shrine |image = [[File:Shiro_Jinja.jpg|280px|alt=Shiro Shrine's Worship Hall and main hall]] |location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = Kotoshironushi no Mikoto (and others) |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival) |rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is **Kotoshironushi no Mikoto**, among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * **main hall (Honden)**: Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * **Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest**: A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * **New Year's festival (January 1)** * **Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)** * **Autumn festival (October 20)**: Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == ### Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation) * **Shiro Shrine Offerings**: Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * **location**: Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * **Transport**: * Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. * A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shiro Shrine |image = [[File:Shiro_Jinja.jpg|280px|alt=Shiro Shrine's Worship Hall and main hall]] |location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = Kotoshironushi no Mikoto (and others) |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival) |rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is **Kotoshironushi no Mikoto**, among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * **main hall (Honden)**: Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * **Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest**: A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * **New Year's festival (January 1)** * **Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)** * **Autumn festival (October 20)**: Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == ### Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation) * **Shiro Shrine Offerings**: Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * **location**: Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * **Transport**: * Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. * A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shiro Shrine |image = [[File:Shiro_Jinja.jpg|280px|alt=Shiro Shrine's Worship Hall and main hall]] |location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = Kotoshironushi no Mikoto (and others) |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival) |rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is **Kotoshironushi no Mikoto**, among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * **main hall (Honden)**: Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * **Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest**: A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * **New Year's festival (January 1)** * **Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)** * **Autumn festival (October 20)**: Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation) * **Shiro Shrine Offerings**: Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * **location**: Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * **Transport**: * Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. * A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.

The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.

The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.

|name = Shiro Shrine

|location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan
|coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}}
|deity = Kotoshironushi no Mikoto (and others)
|status = Former Prefectural Shrine
|establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE)
|architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri
|annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival)
|rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival
}}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity ==
The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background ==
The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features ==
* '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable.
* '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals ==
* '''New Year's festival (January 1)'''
* '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)'''
* '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage ==
=== Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)
* '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access ==
* '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan.
* '''Transport''':
* Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]].
* A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References ==
* ''Takebe Town History''
* On-site explanatory panels
== External Links ==
* [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency
* [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

|architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri
|annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival)
|rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival
}}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity ==
The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background ==
The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features ==
* '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable.
* '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals ==
* '''New Year's festival (January 1)'''
* '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)'''
* '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage ==
=== Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)
* '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access ==
* '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan.
* '''Transport''':
* Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]].
* A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References ==
* ''Takebe Town History''
* On-site explanatory panels
== External Links ==
* [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency
* [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|name = Shiro Shrine
|image = [[File:Shiro_Jinja.jpg|280px|alt=Shiro Shrine's Worship Hall and main hall]]
|location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan
|coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}}
|deity = Kotoshironushi no Mikoto (and others)
|status = Former Prefectural Shrine
|establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE)
|architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri
|annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival)
|rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival
}}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity ==
The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background ==
The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features ==
* '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable.
* '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals ==
* '''New Year's festival (January 1)'''
* '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)'''
* '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage ==
=== Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)===
* '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access ==
* '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan.
* '''Transport''':
* Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]].
* A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References ==
* ''Takebe Town History''
* On-site explanatory panels
== External Links ==
* [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency
* [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity ==
The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background ==
The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features ==
* '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable.
* '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals ==
* '''New Year's festival (January 1)'''
* '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)'''
* '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage ==
=== Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)===
* '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access ==
* '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan.
* '''Transport''':
** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]].
** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References ==
* ''Takebe Town History''
* On-site explanatory panels

'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity ==
The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background ==
The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features ==
* '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable.
* '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals ==
* '''New Year's festival (January 1)'''
* '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)'''
* '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage ==
=== Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)===
* '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access ==
* '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan.
* '''Transport''':
** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]].
** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.

|coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}}
|deity = Kotoshironushi no Mikoto (and others)
|status = Former Prefectural Shrine
|establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE)
|architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri
|annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival)
|rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival
}}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity ==
The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background ==
The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features ==
* '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable.
* '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals ==
* '''New Year's festival (January 1)'''
* '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)'''
* '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage ==
=== Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)===
* '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access ==
* '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan.
* '''Transport''':
** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]].
** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References ==
* ''Takebe Town History''
* On-site explanatory panels
== External Links ==
* [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency
* [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

|coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}}
|deity = Kotoshironushi no Mikoto (and others)
|status = Former Prefectural Shrine
|establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE)
|architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri
|annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival)
|rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival
}}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity ==
The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background ==
The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features ==
* '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable.
* '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals ==
* '''New Year's festival (January 1)'''
* '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)'''
* '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage ==
=== Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)===
* '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access ==
* '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan.
* '''Transport''':
** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]].
** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References ==
* ''Takebe Town History''
* On-site explanatory panels
== External Links ==
* [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency
* [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

|annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival)
|rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival
}}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity ==
The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background ==
The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features ==
* '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable.
* '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals ==
* '''New Year's festival (January 1)'''
* '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)'''
* '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage ==
=== Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)===
* '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access ==
* '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan.
* '''Transport''':
** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]].
** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References ==
* ''Takebe Town History''
* On-site explanatory panels
== External Links ==
* [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency
* [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

* '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals ==
* '''New Year's festival (January 1)'''
* '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)'''
* '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage ==
=== Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)===
* '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access ==
* '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan.
* '''Transport''':
** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]].
** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References ==
* ''Takebe Town History''
* On-site explanatory panels
== External Links ==

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shiro Shrine |image = [[File:Shiro_Jinja.jpg|280px|alt=Shiro Shrine's Worship Hall and main hall]] |location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = Kotoshironushi no Mikoto (and others) |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival) |rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[:en:Fukuwatari Station|Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[:en:Fukuwatari Station|Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

revision.comment: 11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:志呂神社 up to rev 89940316
revision.text:
{{神社 |画像 = [[File:Shiro_Jinja.jpg|280px]]
志呂神社の拝殿（手前）と本殿（奥） |名称 = 志呂神社 |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町下神目]]1834 |位置 = |祭神 = [[事代主命]]　ほか |神体 = |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = （伝）[[和銅]]6年（[[713年]]） |本殿 = [[中山造]]の発展形 |別名 = 志呂宮（しろみや） |例祭 = [[10月20日]]（秋祭） |神事 = 歳旦祭、輪潜祭 }} [[ファイル:Entarance_of_Shiro_Jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|志呂神社の表参道（随神門前）]] '''志呂神社'''（しろじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町下神目]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]である。'''志呂宮'''（しろみや）とも呼ばれる。福徳成就・安産・厄除けの福徳があるとされる。 == 祭神 == 祭神は、[[事代主命]]などである。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === [[美作国]]が[[備前国]]から分国した[[和銅]]6年（[[713年]]）に、[[弓削庄]]27カ村（現在の[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町]]のうち旧[[久米郡]][[福渡町]]の範囲、および久米郡[[久米南町]]）の総氏神として祭られたと伝えられている。 == 境内 == * 本殿 : [[中山造]]より発展した3間4面（実尺は4間×4間）の総欅造の出向拝を備えた入母屋造妻入で、各所に彫物が施されている。[[1848年]]（[[嘉永]]元年）再建現地の説明板による。。 * 三樹山（みきやま）の樹林 : 三樹山は、鎮守の森としてこれまでほとんど手が加えられることなく、自然の推移にゆだねられてきた。このため、シイノキ、ヤブツバキ、シリブカガシなど原始的な植生型に近い常緑広葉樹が育っている。常緑広葉樹林では、四季を通じて熱い葉に被われており、林外に比べて気温の変化や湿度の変化が少なく、冬の寒さをきらう南方系と夏の暑さに弱い北方系の動植物が同居する珍しいところである。[[1976年]]（昭和51年）に、岡山県指定郷土自然保護地域となった。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 輪潜祭（7月最終土曜日） * 例祭（秋祭、[[10月20日]]） == 文化財 == === 重要無形民俗文化財（岡山県指定） === * 志呂神社御供 : 通称「京尾御供」と呼ばれ、志呂神社の秋祭（10月20日）に、[[久米南町]]京尾の氏子から供えられる神饌行事である。だんごでつくった「フト」と称する女陰を形どったもの3個と、男根を象徴した「マガリ」1個、餅でつくった「丁銀」3個、それに米飯一盛、柚子1個、箸1膳、榊葉若干を三方に盛った7膳の熟饌が調整され、御幣を立てた唐櫃にいれて御供所に運ばれ、紋付袴に榊葉を口にした7人の頭人によって供饌される。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）指定。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]][[建部町下神目]]1834 {{osm box|n|1907339715}} ; 交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[津山線]][[福渡駅]]から車で10分弱。 * [[国道53号]]志呂神社前バス停から徒歩10分弱。 == 参考文献 == * 『建部町史』 * 現地説明板 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 志呂神社]（岡山県神社庁） * {{Wayback|url=http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html |title=志呂神社 |date=*}}（オープンエア・おかやま） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しろしんしや}} [[Category:岡山市の神社]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形文化財]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:岡山市北区の建築物]]

It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[:en:Fukuwatari Station|Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels

'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[:en:Fukuwatari Station|Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[Tsuyama Line]]. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[:en:Fukuwatari Station|Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]]. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20044988]] Wikidata name: Shiro-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shiro Shrine |image = [[File:Shiro_Jinja.jpg|280px|alt=Shiro Shrine's Worship Hall and main hall]] |location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = Kotoshironushi no Mikoto (and others) |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival) |rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[:en:Fukuwatari Station|Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]]. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:志呂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20044988]] Wikidata name: Shiro-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shiro Shrine |image = File:Shiro Jinja.jpg |location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611}} |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival) |rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q855478}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|qid=Q11364287}} }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[:en:Fukuwatari Station|Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]]. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:志呂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044988]] Wikidata name: Shiro-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shiro Shrine |image = File:Shiro Jinja.jpg |location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}} |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival) |rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|WD=Q11364287}} }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[:en:Fukuwatari Station|Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]]. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)
