<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:志呂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:志呂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:志呂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044988]] Wikidata name: Shiro-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shiro Shrine |image = File:Shiro Jinja.jpg |location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}} |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival) |rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|WD=Q11364287}} }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[:en:Fukuwatari Station|Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]]. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:志呂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:志呂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:志呂神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044988]] Wikidata name: Shiro-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shiro Shrine |image = File:Shiro Jinja.jpg |location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}} |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival) |rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|WD=Q11364287}} }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s [[:en:Fukuwatari Station|Fukuwatari Station]] on the [[:en:Tsuyama Line|Tsuyama Line]]. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)
{{Shinto Horizontal}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shiro Shrine |image = File:Shiro Jinja.jpg |location = Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|N|133|55|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611}} |status = Former Prefectural Shrine |establishment = Traditionally dated to Wadō 6 (713 CE) |architecture = Evolved form of Nakayama-zukuri |annual_festival = October 20 (Autumn festival) |rituals = New Year's festival, Ring Passing festival |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q855478}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Nakayama-zukuri|WD=Q11364287}} }}
'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s {{ill|Fukuwatari Station|en|Fukuwatari Station|WD=Q5507752|lt=Fukuwatari Station}} on the {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line}}. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
== References == * ''Takebe Town History'' * On-site explanatory panels
== External Links == * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=17064 Shiro Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency * [Archived Information on Shiro Shrine](http://www2a.biglobe.ne.jp/marusan/phshirojinjya1.html)
{{Shinto Horizontal}}

'''Shiro Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|志呂神社}}, ''Shiro Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system and is colloquially referred to as ''Shiromiya'' (志呂宮). The shrine is known for blessings of prosperity, safe childbirth, and warding off misfortune.
== Enshrined deity == The primary enshrined deity is '''Kotoshironushi no Mikoto''', among others.
== Historical Background == The shrine is believed to have been established in 713 CE, the year when the Mimasaka Province separated from Bizen Province. It was venerated as the collective tutelary deity of the 27 villages of Yuge-shō, an area that includes the present-day Takebe Town and parts of neighboring Kumenan Town.
== Architectural Features == * '''main hall (Honden)''': Constructed in 1848 (Kaei 1), the main hall represents an evolved form of the Nakayama-zukuri style. It features intricate carvings and a hip-and-gable roof (irimoya-zukuri) with a front-facing gable. * '''Three-Trees Hill (Mikiyama) Forest''': A sacred grove preserved in its natural state, hosting evergreen broad-leaved trees such as chinquapins and camellias. This forest is designated as an Okayama Prefecture Natural Conservation Area.
== festivals and Rituals == * '''New Year's festival (January 1)''' * '''Ring Passing festival (Last Saturday in July)''' * '''Autumn festival (October 20)''': Known for traditional offerings and ceremonies.
== Cultural Heritage == === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Okayama Prefecture Designation)=== * '''Shiro Shrine Offerings''': Also called ''Kyo’o Offerings'', these include symbolic food items like dango shaped as female and male symbols, silver-shaped rice cakes, and various other offerings. Presented during the Autumn festival, the offerings are prepared by representatives from Kyo’o in Kumenan Town. This tradition was designated in 1957.
== Access == * '''location''': Shimokameno 1834, Takebe Town, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. * '''Transport''': ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from JR West’s {{ill|Fukuwatari Station|en|Fukuwatari Station|WD=Q5507752|lt=Fukuwatari Station|1=Fukuwatari Station}} on the {{ill|Tsuyama Line|en|Tsuyama Line|WD=Q7850362|lt=Tsuyama Line|1=Tsuyama Line}}. ** A short walk from Shiro Shrine Mae bus stop on National Route 53.
